event: Portrait of Bodhidharma (Daruma) in Zen ink painting
description: A Japanese Zen ink painting (sumi-e) depicting Bodhidharma (Daruma), the legendary founder of Zen Buddhism, in a highly minimalist style. The figure is reduced to bold, sweeping brushstrokes showing a round face with intense staring eyes, thick eyebrows, a prominent nose, and a beard. His robed body is rendered with just a few curved strokes. Japanese calligraphy fills the upper left portion of the square composition. The painting is mounted on silk in the traditional hanging scroll format. Red seal stamps appear among the calligraphy.
